iterations.description: A/B tests against evals/scaffold (Token contract, no CLAUDE.md/tests/scripts), tools ENABLED, v4.1.0-rc.2

key_findings.best_test: CMP-06 (review-contract): +2.0 — prevents 3 false positives about Aztec internals
false_positives_without_skill: Noir u64 wraps on overflow (FALSE — panics)
double .at() is a bug (FALSE — correct for Owned<PrivateSet>)

iterations.description: Applied eval recommendations: removed 6 low-value skills, kept 2 (review-contract + aztec-developer)
changes: Removed 6 skills with zero or marginal eval delta
Rewrote aztec-developer SKILL.md with 'Common Hallucinations to Avoid' section
Content reduced from 47 files / 7147 lines to 27 files / 2841 lines (60% reduction)
Updated CLAUDE.md and README.md to remove stale references

iterations.description: Full 10-test A/B eval with consolidated 2-skill plugin against scaffold, assertion-graded

key_findings.best_test: CMP-07 (TXE testing): +62.5% — without-skill used deprecated v3 env.impersonate() API
key_findings.issue_found: CMP-06 with-skill still claimed Noir overflow wraps despite skill saying it panics — review-contract missing this fact

changes_applied: Added 'Common False Positives' section to review-contract skill (Noir overflow, note randomness, double .at())
Strengthened MCP verification step in review-contract to be MANDATORY for Critical/High findings

Updated evals.json: CMP-01 accepts SingleUseClaim alternative, CMP-02/03 removed getTimeouts() (non-discriminating), CMP-06 adapted to scaffold Token contract and added overflow/randomness false positive checks

next_steps: Re-run CMP-06 and CMP-01 with updated skills and assertions to verify improvements
Add more framework gotchas to aztec-developer as new hallucinations are discovered
Consider adding E2E task verification (compile + run) as automated eval step
Investigate why with-skill runs use 1.8x more tokens — consider trimming verbose skill references
